topic: A person discovers the tree they planted as a child has been cut down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of damp earth and old bark hit me first, a sudden, sharp jolt in the quiet afternoon. I’d been walking along the edge of the woods, a familiar path I’d taken countless times since I was small enough to bury a sapling myself. But something was wrong. Where the sturdy, young oak, the one I’d painstakingly nurtured for years, should have stood sentinel, there was only a jagged, raw stump. My breath hitched, catching painfully in my throat. I slowly lowered my gaze to the ground, my fingers trembling as they brushed against the disturbed soil. A cold knot tightened in my chest, and I took a shaky step backward, my mind racing through impossible scenarios. Who would have done this? The silence of the forest suddenly felt heavy, oppressive, as if the very air was holding its breath, waiting for me to make a sound.

[story 2]

I found the clearing by accident, drawn by a strange, hollow sound that wasn't the usual rustle of leaves. Then I saw it. The place where I’d planted my first tree, a small, hopeful thing I’d tended with relentless care as a child. Now, it was just a cleared patch, the earth churned up and bare. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my palms sweat instantly. I stood frozen, my feet rooted to the spot, every muscle in my body rigid. My heart began to pound a frantic rhythm against my ribs. I wanted to turn and run, but my legs wouldn't obey. A low, involuntary moan escaped my lips, swallowed quickly by the dense surrounding foliage. I kept staring at the empty space, my eyes wide and unfocused, searching for any sign of life, any indication that this wasn't real.

[story 3]

It was the sight that stole the air from my lungs. Standing where the young maple had been, there was only evidence of violence—a clean, brutal cut through the trunk, leaving behind splintered wood and freshly exposed roots. My vision tunneled slightly. I dropped the small trowel I was holding; it clattered onto the mossy ground, the sound deafening in the stillness. A tremor ran through my entire frame, starting in my hands and vibrating up into my shoulders. I stumbled back, tripping over a root, and landed hard on the uneven ground. I pressed my hands over my mouth, trying to stifle a rising tide of panic that threatened to overwhelm my composure. Every instinct screamed at me to flee, to disappear into the shadows, but I remained rooted, staring at the stark, empty space where my childhood promise had once stood.
